narrative: In 1996, the British Virgin Islands were beset by a storm that brought widespread destruction.
The tempest, though not severe enough to claim any lives, still caused significant damage, estimated at $4 million.
Despite the devastation, the islands' resilience and natural beauty shone through, a testament to their enduring spirit.
2017 saw the British Virgin Islands struck by a powerful storm, leaving 9 people dead.
The storm caused an estimated $383.92 million in damages, affecting the lives of many islanders.
The disaster was a harsh reminder of the region's vulnerability to natural disasters.
